mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a robust labor market and increasing consumer spending capacity, which consistently supports positive equity market performance over the short term as corporate earnings benefit from higher demand.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS value of 131708.0 suggests a large and active labor force.
regime_hint: any regime with positive employment growth
failure_condition: If PAYEMS month-over-month growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is at historically low levels, it signals an accommodative monetary policy that reduces the cost of capital and encourages investment, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS value is 0.08%, indicating highly accommodative policy.
regime_hint: post-recession recovery, low interest rate environment
failure_condition: If, during periods where FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, the average 60-day forward return for S&P 500 is negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS) combined with moderate consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates healthy consumer demand without immediate pressure for monetary tightening, creating a favorable environment for corporate earnings and equity appreciation.
failure_condition: If periods of strong RSAFS growth (e.g., > 0.5% MoM) and moderate CPIAUCSL growth (e.g., < 0.3% MoM) do not yield significantly positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.

mechanism_narrative: In a high unemployment regime, a significant decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals improving economic conditions and increased consumer confidence, which drives equity markets higher as recovery takes hold over a longer horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE is 8.6%, indicating a high unemployment regime.
failure_condition: If, when UNRATE is above 7% and declines by more than 0.5 percentage points over three months, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing and overall economic activity, which translates into higher corporate revenues and supports positive equity market performance over the very short term.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a key coincident indicator of economic health.
regime_hint: any regime with positive industrial output
failure_condition: If INDPRO month-over-month growth is positive but S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust construction activity and future economic growth, boosting investor confidence and leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts are a leading indicator of economic activity.
failure_condition: If, following a month-over-month increase in HOUST greater than 10%, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of very low federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) and high unemployment (UNRATE) suggests a period of significant monetary stimulus aimed at job creation, which eventually translates into stronger economic activity and higher equity valuations over a longer horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS is 0.08% and UNRATE is 8.6%.
regime_hint: deep monetary easing during high unemployment
failure_condition: If, during periods where FEDFUNDS is below 0.5% and UNRATE is above 7%, the average 60-day forward return for S&P 500 is negative or not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong economic growth, a sustained increase in GDP (GDPC1) signals robust corporate earnings and investor confidence, leading to continued positive momentum in the S&P 500 over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP growth is a primary indicator of economic health.
failure_condition: If, during periods of GDPC1 growth above 2.5%, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of observations over any 5-year rolling window.
